question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 lowers MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (R2↔R3 IPv6 core) from 1514
to 1400. R3 stays at default. IS-IS IPv6 DBD/LSP exchange fails for
oversize packets; adjacency stalls in INIT. SRv6 underlay reachability
between PE1 and PE2 breaks. VPNv4 iBGP over IPv6 drops once the IGP
no longer carries fc00::4.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R2, the MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R2<->R3 IPv6 core) was lowered to 1400 while R3 stays at 1514.
The asymmetric L3 MTU means oversize IS-IS IPv6 DBD/LSP packets cannot traverse the link, so the two ends cannot complete database synchronization and the IS-IS adjacency cannot form. Without the adjacency the SRv6 underlay between PE1 and PE2 has no reachability, and once the IGP no longer carries fc00::4 the VPNv4-over-IPv6 iBGP session cannot stay up, so VPN prefixes stop propagating.
Fix: remove the MTU override on R2's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (restore 1514).
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; the
diagnostic ground truth lives in expected_state_diffs (CLI
probe outputs the agent should observe).
expected_state_diffs: R2 `show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: MTU 1400 (baseline: 1514). R3's Gi0/0/0/0: still default — asymmetric L3 MTU on a point-to-point link.
R2 `show isis interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: per-interface state may flag MTU mismatch; full-size IS-IS LSPs from R3 are dropped by R2.
Any router `ping ipv6 fc00::3 source Loopback0 size 1500`: fragmentation-needed / drops, while `size 1000` succeeds — asymmetric MTU breaks large-packet traversal across this link.
Diff `show running-config interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0` between R2 and R3: only R2 carries the lowered MTU.

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no mtu
